description_long: This is a vintage Sony radio receiver with a rectangular wooden enclosure that shows signs of wear and age, including visible scratches and scuffs. The front face features a large black speaker grille on the left, a frequency display panel with FM and AM bands, and two prominent circular dials for tuning and volume control. The top of the radio has two rectangular buttons, likely for power and band selection, set into the wood surface. The sides and back are plain, with the wood grain and aged finish continuing around the entire body. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, with a nostalgic aesthetic typical of mid-20th-century consumer electronics. The radio is compact and portable, suitable for placement on a table, shelf, or workbench in an urban environment.
description: A vintage Sony radio receiver with a worn wooden case, large black speaker grille, frequency display, and two control dials on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black speaker grille, frequency display, and two control dials, with the Sony logo visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the plain wooden surface and the top-mounted buttons, with no visible controls or speaker.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the unadorned wooden back, highlighting the aged and scratched finish.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain wooden surface and a partial view of the top buttons.
